name: Cefapirin
description: Cefapirin (INN, also spelled cephapirin) is an injectable, first-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. It is marketed under the trade name Cefadyl. Production for use in humans has been discontinued in the United States. Cefapirin is partly plasma-bound and is effective against gram-negative and gram-positive organisms.
indication: For treatment of infections caused by susceptible bacteria.
toxicity: Rats exposed via the oral route to cephapirin displayed low acute toxicity (LD<sub>50</sub> = 14000 mg/kg). The most common adverse reactions are hypersensitivity reactions and alterations to liver function. Evidence of white blood cell disorders and anaemia were noted in some subjects.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal activity of cephapirin results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis via affinity for penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs).
pharmacodynamics: Cephapirin is a first-generation cephalosporin that has a wide spectrum of activity against gram-positive and gram-negative organisms. Cephapirin is more resistant to beta-lactamases than are the penicillins and so is effective against staphylococci, with the exception of methicillin-resistant staphylococci.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefapirin.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefapirin.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefapirin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefapirin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
